posts.title: Your Landlord Won't Fix Things? Here's What to Do
posts.description: Broken heater, leaking pipes, mold — and your landlord ignores your requests. Here's how to escalate repairs legally, whether you're on Section 8 or not.

posts.title: Moving to a New Place With Your Section 8 Voucher
posts.description: You can move with Section 8 — even to another state. Here's how portability works, what paperwork you need, and the timeline for transferring your voucher.

posts.title: What Counts as Income on Section 8? The Complete List
posts.description: Not all money counts as income for Section 8. Here's what your PHA includes, what's excluded, and the deductions that can lower your rent portion.

posts.title: How Section 8 Inspections Work (And How to Pass Yours)
posts.description: HQS inspections can make or break your housing search. Here's exactly what inspectors look for, common fail items, and how to prepare so your unit passes the first time.

posts.title: Renting With an Eviction on Your Record: What Actually Works
posts.description: An eviction on your record makes finding housing harder, but not impossible. Strategies for getting approved, finding flexible landlords, and clearing your record.

posts.title: My Income Went Up on Section 8 — Will I Lose My Voucher?
posts.description: Getting a raise on Section 8 doesn't mean losing your voucher. Here's exactly what happens to your rent, when to report changes, and how to plan for the benefits cliff.

posts.title: I Need a Place to Stay Tonight — Here's What to Do
posts.description: If you need emergency housing right now, here are the phone numbers to call, what to expect at a shelter, and what to do if shelters are full.

posts.title: How to Survive the Section 8 Waiting List
posts.description: The Section 8 wait can last years. Here's how to stay on the list, explore alternatives, and keep moving forward while you wait for your voucher.

posts.title: Reasonable Accommodations: What They Are and How to Get One
posts.description: If you have a disability, you have the legal right to request changes to housing rules and policies. Here's how reasonable accommodations work and how to ask for one.

posts.title: What Happens When Your Section 8 Voucher Expires
posts.description: Your Section 8 voucher has an expiration date. Here's what happens when time runs out, how to get an extension, and what to do if you've already lost your voucher.

posts.title: Why Housing Changes Everything
posts.description: Housing isn't just a roof over your head. It's the foundation that makes recovery, employment, safety, and stability possible. Real stories from a HUD-VASH case worker.

posts.title: 15 Tips for Finding an Apartment with a Housing Voucher
posts.description: Practical, actionable tips for finding an apartment when you have a Section 8 voucher. From searching strategies to dealing with landlord rejection.

posts.title: What Housing First Actually Means (And Why It Works)
posts.description: Understanding the Housing First model — why it works better than treatment-first approaches, how HUD-VASH uses it, and what it means for people experiencing homelessness.

posts.title: Your PHA Won't Call Back? Here's What to Do
posts.description: Practical strategies for reaching your housing authority when they're not responding. We've been there too. Here's how to get their attention and move things forward.

posts.title: The Benefits Cliff: What It Is and How to Plan for It
posts.description: Understanding the benefits cliff — how a raise or new job can suddenly reduce your assistance. We explain which programs have cliffs, and strategies to manage them.

posts.title: 7 Section 8 Myths That Need to Die
posts.description: The truth about Section 8 vouchers. Debunking common myths that keep people from accessing the housing help they qualify for.

posts.title: What Happens After You Get Your Housing Voucher
posts.description: The apartment search with a Section 8 voucher is different from renting without one. Here's what to expect: timelines, inspections, landlord negotiations, and what to do when doors close in your face.

posts.title: Talk to Your Landlord About Repairs Safely
posts.description: Your landlord has a legal obligation to maintain your rental. Here's how to document problems, request repairs, put it in writing, and protect yourself if they retaliate.

posts.title: What to Do If Your Benefits Get Cut
posts.description: Your SSI, SSDI, SNAP, or housing benefits got reduced or terminated. Here's what to do immediately, your appeal rights, timelines, and where to find help.

posts.title: Utility Allowances Explained — How They Affect Your Rent
posts.description: What are utility allowances in Section 8 and public housing? Learn how they work, how they lower your rent, and what to do if your real bills are higher.

posts.title: What to Do When a Landlord Rejects Your Voucher
posts.description: Landlord won't accept your Section 8 voucher? Here's what you can do about it — your rights, your options, and how to keep moving forward.

posts.title: 5 Lesser-Known Affordable Housing Programs
posts.description: Section 8 isn't your only option. Here are five federal housing programs that could help you — and that most people have never heard of.

posts.title: Section 8 Explained — What It Is & How It Works
posts.description: A clear, no-nonsense breakdown of the Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher program — what it is, who qualifies, and how the process actually works from someone who's seen it firsthand.

posts.title: Welcome to Waythrough Project
posts.description: Why I built Waythrough Project, how the resource finder works, and what you'll find here — free guides, tools, templates, and state-by-state resources for navigating affordable housing.
